_calibration: CALIBRATION FIXTURE (known-bad, deliberately committed): the earn-your-zero regex-health class (caution.md C-050), Compliance Object Model shape with a detection.pattern field (the future post-migration shape this linter is written ahead of). In this JSON source the pattern reads dpo[@\\s] which, once JSON-decoded, compiles to the character class {@, backslash, s} - it can never match a literal whitespace after 'dpo' as the rule author intended, only a literal '@', backslash or 's'. The seeded positive_example ('...contact our dpo team...') has a SPACE after 'dpo', so the pattern does NOT match its own positive_example even though it compiles cleanly: a dead-on-arrival pattern with a proof case attached, the exact over-escaped C-050 class. catalogue/linters/regex-health.js MUST flag this as regex-dead-pattern in --calibrate mode, or it has not earned its zero.

records.name: UK GDPR Article 37 DPO contact details (calibration fixture: dead over-escaped regex)
applies_when: required to designate a Data Protection Officer
excluded_when: not required to designate a DPO

website_obligations.duty: Publish the Data Protection Officer's contact details

detection.positive_example: for privacy queries please contact our dpo team directly

sources: calibration fixture (deliberately seeded dead over-escaped regex)
